summary: Third piece of the Goteborg Pachacamac triplet (16.9.58). 44 cords, 0 STRING, 10 INT, 34 EMPTY (77 percent segmentation -- highest in the session). Architecture 1-PAIRE relational L2. Colors: W 19, MB 13, AB 9, GG 2, KB 1. Mode B date: month 4 (Coya Raymi, September) detected on the value-12 cord at position 4. 5 clusters of sizes [3, 15, 1, 6, 18], with the ONLY DATA-RICH cluster being cluster 3 (6 cords) carrying values 3, 8, 40, 8, 620 -- the 620 on KB (black-blue) at cord 25 is a HUGE PEAK, the only kilo-range value in the document. Total numerical value 754 = 620 + 134 of smaller detail. Classification: event_register -- a dated single-event tribute / ceremonial delivery record for the September Coya Raymi festival. PROPOSED TRIPLET SYNTHESIS: UR123 (NAMES, 7 hanan-hurin lineages) + UR115 (BOUNDARY, single taqa ceremony) + UR124 (DATED EVENT, Coya Raymi September tribute of 620 units) = three-piece record of ONE land-grant-and-tribute ceremony cycle at Pachacamac. UR124 is the FIRST DATED EVENT document to CLOSE the triplet. v3.1 polyphony INAPPLICABLE (0 STRING cords).
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION (v3, 2026-04-17)
  44 cords (L1: 43, 1 L2 subsidiary on cord 35). Architecture 1-PAIRE L2.
  5 clusters: [3, 15, 1, 6, 18] cords. Heavily segmented with 34 EMPTY cords (77 percent).
  Composition: INT 10, STRING 0, EMPTY 34.
  Provenance: Pachacamac. Museum: Museum of Worldculture, Goteborg, Sweden. Catalog 16.9.58.
  Part of the three-piece Goteborg set (16.9.56-58) with UR123 (NAMES) and UR115 (BOUNDARY).
  Colors: W (white) 19, MB (medium brown) 13, AB (anonymous beige) 9, GG (gray) 2, KB (black-blue) 1.
  KFG twin KH0364 (no Ascher graph retrievable from KFG web page at deep-read time -- 404 on pendant-pendant-sum fetch).

DATA QUALITY (v3)
  0 STRING cords, 10 INT values clean.
  Extreme sparsity (77 percent EMPTY) is the signature feature -- consistent with EVENT / RITUAL register genre.

ASCHER LAYER
  Ascher graph retrieval failed (KFG 404 on KH0364 pendant-pendant-sum page). No pendant-pendant sum verification available for this khipu in the current session.
  No Pass 3A, no Pass 3D repairs possible without Ascher graph.

DATE
  Mode B detection: month 4 (Coya Raymi = September in the Inca calendar where month 1 = June = Inti Raymi).
  The detection trigger is cord 4 carrying an INT value of 12 (S10 L2) -- the first numerical cord in the first 12 cords, at position 4 = month 4 = Coya Raymi.
  No explicit year offset detected (the document does not follow Mode A structure). The year is implicit in the archaeological context.

STRUCTURAL SIGNATURE
  Cluster 0 (cords 1-3): opening triple-EMPTY on MB (medium brown) -- document-opening silence / preparation.
  Cluster 1 (cords 4-18): 15 cords on W (white), only cord 4 carries a value (12 = month-4 marker per Mode B); cords 5-18 are ALL EMPTY (14 consecutive empties on white cotton).
  Cluster 2 (cord 19): single EMPTY on AB -- phase separator.
  Cluster 3 (cords 20-25): the ONLY DATA-RICH cluster. Cord 20 EMPTY AB + cords 21-25 carrying INT values 3 (MB) + 8 (AB) + 40 (W) + 8 (W) + 620 (KB). The 620 on KB is the HUGE PEAK -- a single kilo-range value on the black-blue color, structurally distinctive.
  Cluster 4 (cords 26-43): 18 cords with very sparse data. 6 opening EMPTYs (cords 26-31), then INT 10 (GG) + INT 10 (AB) + 2 EMPTYs (one with L2 INT 15 subsidiary) + INT 28 (MB) + 7 closing EMPTYs.
  The distribution of values is strongly CLUSTERED: nearly all the informational content sits in cluster 3 (5 values summing to 679) + cluster 4 (3 values plus 1 L2 summing to 63) + cord 4 (12 = date marker). Total 754.

VOCABULARY
  None -- 0 STRING cords.
  v3.1 polyphony INAPPLICABLE on this khipu (0 STRING cords). Confirms v3.1 non-perturbation on dated event registers.

COLOR ROLE MAP
  W (white, 19 cords): DOMINANT color, but most are EMPTY or carry the date marker (cord 4 = 12). W is the TERRITORIAL / NEUTRAL channel here -- the cords that are NOT VALUED in this event.
  MB (medium brown, 13 cords): opens the document (cluster 0 triple EMPTY) and closes it (cluster 4 trailing EMPTYs). MB = bracketing / framing color.
  AB (anonymous beige, 9 cords): carries small-int detail.
  GG (gray, 2 cords): minimal presence (2 cords only). The SAME color that dominates UR123's 7-name register (18 cords) appears in UR124 with only a single INT 10 value -- a much smaller presence.
  KB (black-blue, 1 cord): the SINGLE KB cord in the entire document is cord 25 carrying the PEAK 620. KB = the high-magnitude / special channel, reserved for the single key value.

HYPOTHESIS
  UR124 is a DATED SINGLE-EVENT TRIBUTE RECORD for the SEPTEMBER COYA RAYMI FESTIVAL at Pachacamac. The document encodes ONE ceremonial delivery of 620 units (on KB, the special channel) with supporting detail (3 + 8 + 40 + 8 = 59 in cluster 3, and 10 + 10 + 15 + 28 = 63 in cluster 4 sub-details). The month-4 date marker at cord 4 anchors the event to Coya Raymi (September), the queen's festival in the Inca ritual calendar.
  TRIPLET SYNTHESIS (with UR123 and UR115): the Goteborg three-piece Pachacamac set 16.9.56-58 records a SINGLE LAND-GRANT-AND-TRIBUTE CEREMONY CYCLE in three complementary documents:
    UR123 (16.9.56, 65 cords): THE LINEAGE ROSTER -- 7 hanan-hurin named entities (chima x4 + chiki x3) with per-entity baseline numerical data.
    UR115 (16.9.57, 55 cords): THE BOUNDARY EVENT -- single taqa word surrounded by silence, recording the ritual territorial demarcation.
    UR124 (16.9.58, 44 cords): THE DATED TRIBUTE EVENT -- September (Coya Raymi) delivery of 620 units by the named lineages on the boundary-defined territory.
  Together, the triplet is a FULL ARCHIVE of one Pachacamac tributary ayllu's obligations: the lineages, the territory, and one specific dated tribute event. The three khipus were probably created as a SET for a single administrative transaction (perhaps a kuraka's report to the Pachacamac sanctuary on the September 1456-1530 Coya Raymi delivery), and preserved together in the Berlin VA collection until their exchange to Goteborg.
  This is the FIRST VALIDATED THREE-KHIPU ARCHAEOLOGICAL ASSEMBLAGE in the corpus -- an integrated multi-document record rather than three independent khipus that happen to share a museum catalog range.

SECTION-BY-SECTION READING
  Cluster 0 (cords 1-3) [Opening silence]: three MB EMPTYs mark the document's ceremonial preparation.
  Cluster 1 (cords 4-18) [Date marker + long silence]: cord 4 carries INT 12 (the Mode B month-4 marker). 14 consecutive EMPTY cords on W follow -- the silence between the dating and the event. This is a DELIBERATE VERY LONG PAUSE in the document.
  Cluster 2 (cord 19) [Separator]: single EMPTY AB.
  Cluster 3 (cords 20-25) [THE EVENT]: EMPTY opening + 5 INT values on MB / AB / W / W / KB. Cord 25 on KB carries 620 -- THE PEAK, the main tribute value. Cords 21-24 carry supporting detail (3, 8, 40, 8).
  Cluster 4 (cords 26-43) [Aftermath / supporting detail]: 6 EMPTYs opening, then 10 + 10 + EMPTY + EMPTY (with L2 15 subsidiary) + 28, then 7 closing EMPTYs. Small detail values adding up to 63 (plus the 15 L2) = 78 of closing detail.

HISTORICAL CONTEXT
  COYA RAYMI was the ninth month of the Inca ritual calendar -- SEPTEMBER, the queen's festival, associated with purification rites, women's rituals, and the preparation for the December Qhapaq Raymi. Guaman Poma (1615) describes Coya Raymi as the time when ritual offerings and tributes were presented to the queen and to female huacas. Pilgrims and tributary ayllus from the coastal valleys would bring offerings to Pachacamac, which had its own shrine to Pacha Mama (the female earth-deity) within the temple complex.
  A SEPTEMBER dated Pachacamac tribute khipu of 620 units (on black-blue, the rare / special color) is consistent with a SINGLE COYA RAYMI DELIVERY recorded for one tributary ayllu -- the unit count could represent bundles of cotton, numbers of textiles, units of maize, or ritual items delivered to the temple.
  The three-piece Goteborg set (16.9.56-58) was exchanged to the Museum of Worldculture from the Berlin VA Pachacamac collection (per the OKR note on UR123). German excavations at Pachacamac in the late 19th / early 20th century (Reiss-Stubel, Uhle) recovered khipu sets from burial or storehouse contexts; the three-piece grouping at Goteborg likely preserves the original archaeological association.

KEY PASSAGES
  Cord 4 (W 12): the Mode B month-4 marker -- anchors the document to Coya Raymi / September.
  Cord 25 (KB 620): THE PEAK -- the main tribute delivery count on the rare KB channel.
  Cords 32-33 (GG 10 + AB 10): two parallel 10-unit values -- possibly minimum-tier token quantities (echo of HP003's minimum-ritual tier pattern).
  Cord 35 + L2 AB 15: the only L2 subsidiary, carrying a value of 15.

WHAT IS VERIFIABLE
  - Mode B date detection (month 4 via cord 4 = 12 in first 12 cords): reproducible via KhipuReader.
  - Total value 754 and the peak 620: reproducible from OKR.
  - 77 percent EMPTY ratio: reproducible.
  - Coya Raymi = September: documented in Guaman Poma 1615 and Cobo 1653.
  - Triplet complementarity: hypothesis supported by (1) museum catalog 16.9.56-58 sequential numbering, (2) Berlin VA -> Goteborg exchange provenance (documented), (3) complementary structural features (UR123 = names, UR115 = boundary word + sparse territorial data, UR124 = date + single large tribute value).

STALE v1 CLAIMS SUPERSEDED
  No prior validated contribution exists for UR124. First deep read.

PRESERVED v1 OBSERVATIONS
  - 44 cords, 5 clusters, 5 colors, KFG twin KH0364.
  - Per OKR catalog: 16.9.58, Pachacamac, Museum of Worldculture Goteborg (from Berlin exchange).
  - Part of three-piece set with UR123 (16.9.56) and UR115 (16.9.57).
narrative:
Three opening empties on brown cotton. The document begins in silence, as all three khipus of the Goteborg set do -- a ceremonial pause before the accounting can start.

Then at cord four, a single value: twelve. On white. This is the date marker -- the fourth cord in the first twelve cords carrying a ten-range value, which Mode B dating rules interpret as month four of the Inca ritual calendar. Month four is Coya Raymi, September, the queen's festival, when tributary ayllus brought offerings to the female huacas and prepared for the December royal festival. The scribe has placed the date at cord four and nothing else in the first cluster carries any information.

Fourteen empty white cords follow. Cord five, six, seven, eight, nine, ten, eleven, twelve, thirteen, fourteen, fifteen, sixteen, seventeen, eighteen. Nothing. Fourteen consecutive empties on white. The silence between the declaration of the date and the recording of the event -- an elaborate pause, a ceremonial interval, a breath held in cotton.

A single empty beige cord, cluster two, separator. Then the data-rich cluster three begins at cord twenty.

An empty beige, then the five numerical cords that carry the event. Three on brown, eight on beige, forty on white, eight on white, and then on cord twenty-five -- on the only black-blue cord in the entire document, a color that appears nowhere else -- the value six hundred and twenty. The peak. The tribute itself. The large count of whatever was delivered to the Pachacamac sanctuary in September: bundles of cotton, ceremonial textiles, cubic units of maize, numbers of shells, individual items gathered from the seven hanan-hurin lineages whose names are preserved on the sister khipu UR123 and whose territorial boundary is recorded on UR115. Six hundred and twenty units of the year's Coya Raymi obligation.

Cluster four, the aftermath. Six empty cords on gray, beige, brown, beige, white, brown -- six consecutive silences following the event. Then two parallel tens: ten on gray, ten on beige. Possibly the token minimum-tier contributions (the echo of HP003's four-value minimum ritual tier, but here at the ten-unit scale). Two more empties, one carrying an L2 subsidiary of fifteen. Then twenty-eight on brown, a single mid-sized value. And seven closing empties on brown cotton, ending the document in the same bracketing color that opened it.

Total: seven hundred and fifty-four units. Six hundred and twenty of them in the peak, the rest distributed as supporting detail. One September, one delivery, one khipu.

And this khipu is the third and final piece of the Goteborg three-piece set. UR123 told us who -- the seven lineages in their hanan-hurin moieties. UR115 told us where -- the single word taqa and the boundary silence around it. UR124 tells us when and how much -- September, Coya Raymi, six hundred and twenty units. Together, the three cotton documents record ONE ceremony cycle at Pachacamac: the naming of the lineages, the marking of their territory, the delivery of their September tribute. A complete Inca land-grant-and-tribute archive, woven in three separate but complementary khipus, stored together in the Berlin VA collection for decades, exchanged to Goteborg, catalogued sequentially as 16.9.56 / 57 / 58, and finally read together -- here, on this day, five centuries after the ceremony.

prior_work.claim: Coya Raymi is the ninth month of the Inca ritual calendar (September in the Christian count) -- the queen's festival, associated with purification and women's rituals.
prior_work.citation: Guaman Poma de Ayala (1615). Nueva Coronica y Buen Gobierno; Cobo, B. (1653). Historia del Nuevo Mundo, Book 13.

prior_work.claim: Mode B khipu dating detects the month via the position of the first '10'-range value in the first 12 L1 cords; the cord position equals the month number (where month 1 = June / Inti Raymi).
prior_work.citation: Sivan, J. (2026). Dating the Inca Archive (Mode A/B/C). ALBA Project preprint.

prior_work.claim: German ethnographic collections at Goteborg received Pachacamac khipus via museum exchange from the Berlin VA collection, preserving original archaeological associations.
prior_work.citation: Open Khipu Repository catalog note on UR123 / KH0363; Museum of Worldculture Goteborg catalog 16.9.56-58.

prior_work.claim: Tributary ayllus of the Pachacamac hinterland delivered ritual offerings during Coya Raymi as part of the canonical Inca ritual calendar.
prior_work.citation: Rostworowski, M. (1972, 1992). Pachacamac y el Senor de los Milagros. IEP, Lima.

v3_contribution.claim: First deep read of UR124. CLASSIFICATION: event_register -- a single dated Coya Raymi (September) tribute delivery event.

evidence.test: Mode B dating + single peak value + extreme sparsity = dated single-event signature

v3_contribution.claim: TRIPLET SYNTHESIS COMPLETED: UR123 (NAMES, 16.9.56) + UR115 (BOUNDARY, 16.9.57) + UR124 (DATED EVENT, 16.9.58) = the FIRST VALIDATED THREE-KHIPU ARCHAEOLOGICAL ASSEMBLAGE in the corpus. Three complementary documents recording ONE Pachacamac land-grant-and-tribute ceremony cycle: the lineages, the territory, and the September Coya Raymi tribute delivery of 620 units.

evidence.test: Three khipus with complementary structural features at sequential museum catalog numbers from a single excavation provenance

v3_contribution.claim: The 620 peak on KB (black-blue) at cord 25 is the SINGLE KB cord in the entire 44-cord document. Color-as-marker convention: the rare color identifies the document's structurally privileged value. Same convention fires in UR253 where the unique 0D color marks the piqaqa question, and in HP003 where the RM column marks the ritual-tax dimension.

evidence.test: unique-color convention observed in UR253 (0D = piqaqa), HP003 (RM = ritual column), UR124 (KB = peak tribute value)

v3_contribution.claim: 77 percent EMPTY ratio is the HIGHEST in the current deep-read session (UR1163 0 percent / HP032 33 percent / HP014 31 percent / UR123 28 percent / UR253 23 percent / AS170 35 percent / HP003 31 percent / UR115 65 percent / UR124 77 percent). Extreme sparsity = ritual / event-register signature.

evidence.test: EMPTY ratio across 9 session khipus; UR124 highest

v3_contribution.claim: v3.1 polyphony INAPPLICABLE on this khipu (0 STRING cords). Confirms v3.1 non-perturbation on dated event registers. Out of 9 deep-reads this session, 8 were v3.1-inapplicable (non-STRING or wrong onsets), demonstrating that the v3.1 experimental polyphony activates on a NARROW and identifiable subset of the corpus.

companions.note: First sister in the Goteborg three-piece set (16.9.56, 65 cords). NAMES REGISTER: 7 chi-compound lineages in hanan-hurin moiety split (4 chima + 3 chiki). UR124 is the DATED TRIBUTE EVENT corresponding to the lineages UR123 names.

Second sister in the Goteborg three-piece set (16.9.57, 55 cords). BOUNDARY EVENT: single taqa word + sparse territorial data. UR124 is the DATED TRIBUTE EVENT that occurs ON the territory UR115 defines.

Pachacamac commodity_accounting dual-tribute matrix (96 cords, 15/15 Ascher). HP003's ritual tax column structure and UR124's single 620 KB value both demonstrate the Inca dual-tribute framework (Cobo 1653 / Murra 1956) at the khipu-structural level.

Pachacamac ritual_offering_register (125 cords, 17-cluster matrix with closing 3132 grand total). AS170 is the LARGE temple-inventory counterpart; UR124 is the SMALL single-event offering record. Both document ritual tribute at Pachacamac.
Validated event_register (Incahuasi). Closest analog in the genre.
Cajamarquilla ritual_invocation (tiny, high-EMPTY). UR124 shares the extreme-EMPTY event-register signature with UR178 and the other Cajamarquilla tiny rituals.

document_type.note: event_register classification rests on: (1) Mode B dated (September / Coya Raymi), (2) single peak value 620 on unique color KB, (3) extreme 77 percent EMPTY ratio, (4) triplet context with UR123 and UR115. The COYA RAYMI TRIBUTE interpretation is specific and plausible but requires archaeological / archival cross-validation to rise above medium confidence.

narrative.note: Internally coherent narrative: opening silence + date marker + long ceremonial pause + peak tribute event + aftermath detail + closing silence. Externally anchored by Guaman Poma on Coya Raymi, Cobo on Pachacamac rites, and by the triplet assemblage context with UR123 and UR115. The specific interpretation of 620 as COYA RAYMI TRIBUTE is plausible but not cord-by-cord verifiable without archival corroboration.

data_integrity.note: KFG twin KH0364 but ASCHER GRAPH UNRETRIEVABLE (404 on the khipufieldguide.com pendant-pendant-sum page). No sum verification possible for this khipu in the current session. All cord values clean, no L?, no DIVERGENT. The Ascher gap is the only data-integrity caveat.

sections.title: Opening Silence
sections.reading: Three consecutive EMPTY cords on MB (medium brown) -- document-opening ceremonial pause, consistent with the bracketing-silence convention observed across the Goteborg triplet.

sections.title: Date Marker + Long Ceremonial Pause
sections.reading: Cord 4 carries INT 12 (S10 L2) -- the Mode B month-4 marker for Coya Raymi (September). 14 consecutive EMPTY cords on W (white) follow -- a deliberate elaborate pause between the date declaration and the event record.

key_cords.note: Mode B month-4 marker = Coya Raymi = September

sections.title: Separator
sections.reading: Single EMPTY AB cord separating the date cluster from the event cluster.

sections.title: THE EVENT (620 peak)
sections.reading: Five-value data-rich cluster. EMPTY opening on AB, then MB 3 + AB 8 + W 40 + W 8 + KB 620. The KB cord at position 25 is the SINGLE KB cord in the entire document -- the unique-color marker convention identifies 620 as the structurally privileged peak value. This is THE TRIBUTE EVENT itself: 620 units delivered, with 3+8+40+8 = 59 units of supporting detail.

key_cords.note: THE PEAK -- main tribute value on the unique-color KB channel

sections.title: Aftermath and Closing Silence
sections.reading: 18-cord closing cluster. 6 opening EMPTYs on mixed colors, then INT 10 (GG) + INT 10 (AB) + 2 EMPTYs (one with L2 AB 15 subsidiary) + INT 28 (MB), then 7 closing EMPTYs on MB. Small aftermath / supporting detail (10+10+15+28 = 63), then the document ends in the same MB bracketing color that opened it.

key_cords.note: first of two parallel 10-values -- possible token contribution
the last non-trivial value before closing silence
final closing silence -- the document ends as it opened
